item.title: Caerphilly MP objects to Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has today submitted an objection letter opposing plans to build a new McDonalds drive-thru restaurant in Maesycwmmer. The planned development, which is off Main Road (A472) Maesycwmmer, would see a new McDonalds drive-thru and restaurant built on the site of the former Maesycwmmer Service Station. In his […]

The post Caerphilly MP objects to Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has today submitted an objection letter opposing plans to build a new McDonalds drive-thru restaurant in Maesycwmmer.

The planned development, which is off Main Road (A472) Maesycwmmer, would see a new McDonalds drive-thru and restaurant built on the site of the former Maesycwmmer Service Station.

In his letter, the Caerphilly MP drew attention to his and residents’ concerns over increased traffic and highway safety concerns, noise, light and litter pollution, inadequate consultation, and impact on the character of the area.

He had previously written to McDonalds UK and Ireland outlining his concerns in November 2025, during the pre-application consultation stage.

Mr. Evans said, “I am objecting to the plans to build a new McDonalds in Maesycwmmer because unfortunately the harms it may cause will significantly outweigh any proposed economic benefits.

“Many people in the community have been clear that they have serious and reasonable concerns about this, and I agree with them. This is already a traffic bottleneck and it simply won’t be safe for the road to take more. There will also be noise, light and litter pollution from the proposed restaurant.

“I would encourage anyone who has not yet taken part in the consultation to read the planning documents on the Caerphilly County Borough Council website, and submit their comments.”

The planning application (Reference: 25/0888/FULL) is currently out for public consultation until the 26th February 2026.

The full letter can be read below:

The post Caerphilly MP objects to Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Local MP wins prestigious book award
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
A book authored by the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has won an award at the tenth Westminster Book Awards. The book, The Football Batallions: The Elite Footballers Who Fought in the Great War, won in the category of Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian Category at an event in Parliament on […]

The post Local MP wins prestigious book award appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
A book authored by the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has won an award at the tenth Westminster Book Awards.

The book, The Football Batallions: The Elite Footballers Who Fought in the Great War, won in the category of Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian Category at an event in Parliament on 4th February.

It tells the extraordinary story of well-known footballers who served in the first world war detailing their individual war stories and, for those who returned, their post-war careers.

The Westminster Book Awards are presented by the Booksellers Association and Publishers Association. They recognise excellent political writing across several categories, including Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian, Best Biography by a Parliamentarian, and Best Political Book by a Non-Parliamentarian.

Parliamentarians voted to decide the winner in each category, among the books shortlisted in each category.

Mr. Evans said, “I was very proud to have been shortlisted for this award, and pleased to have won in my category. There were many fantastic books shortlisted, and I want to congratulate them and the other winners.

“It was an honour to research and tell the story of the extraordinary footballers who fought in the First World War.

“They served their communities and their countries on the football pitch, and in the trenches, and we must remember the sacrifices they and so many others made during the Great War.”

The post Local MP wins prestigious book award appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP to meet with UK Minister after ‘Owain’s Law’ debate
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, and Ellie James will meet with Ashley Dalton MP, Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Public Health and Prevention at the UK Government, to discuss the Owain’s Law campaign. The Minister agreed to meet Mr. Evans during a Westminster Hall debate the Caerphilly MP led this morning on […]

The post Caerphilly MP to meet with UK Minister after ‘Owain’s Law’ debate appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, and Ellie James will meet with Ashley Dalton MP, Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Public Health and Prevention at the UK Government, to discuss the Owain’s Law campaign.

The Minister agreed to meet Mr. Evans during a Westminster Hall debate the Caerphilly MP led this morning on the motion ‘That this House has considered the potential merits of equal patient access to tissue freezing for advanced brain cancer treatment, diagnostics and research’.

Opening the debate, Mr. Evans drew attention to the case of Owain James, from Caerphilly, who died in June 2024 at the age of 35 after a glioblastoma tumour was found in his brain. As part of his treatment, brain tissue was removed but only a small amount was kept in a way which could have limited options for further treatment.

His widow, Ellie James, is campaigning for Owain’s Law which would require NHS hospitals freeze suitable brain tumour tissue the right way, and guarantee that the tissue stays the patient’s property under informed consent.

Mr Evans called on the UK Government to work with the Welsh Government and use the UK Government’s upcoming National Cancer Plan in England to introduce more freezers to improve frozen tissue capacity, change attitudes and guidance protocols to make tissue freezing the routine option, clarify the law around tissue ownership, and ensure that people receive a full range of cancer treatment access regardless of where they live.

Mr. Evans said, “Brain cancer is cruel, and one of the deadliest diseases. Patients should not face unacceptable and avoidable barriers to their survival due to outdated and inconsistent practices.

“I am pleased to have been able to lead a debate on Owain’s Law in Parliament, and I want to thank Ellie again for her determination in leading this powerful campaign.

“I look forward to meeting the Minister with Ellie in due course to explore what can be done to make the changes the Owain’s Law campaign are asking for, to help improve cancer outcomes across the UK.”

The post Caerphilly MP to meet with UK Minister after ‘Owain’s Law’ debate appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP secures Owain’s Law Debate in Parliament
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, has secured a Westminster Hall Debate in Parliament on the Owain’s Law campaign. The debate, which will be held in Parliament’s Westminster Hall at 9:30am on 7th January 2026, is on the motion ‘That this House has considered the potential merits of equal patient access to tissue […]

The post Caerphilly MP secures Owain’s Law Debate in Parliament appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, has secured a Westminster Hall Debate in Parliament on the Owain’s Law campaign.

The debate, which will be held in Parliament’s Westminster Hall at 9:30am on 7th January 2026, is on the motion ‘That this House has considered the potential merits of equal patient access to tissue freezing for advanced brain cancer treatment, diagnostics and research’ and will be led by Mr. Evans.

Owain James, from Caerphilly, died in June 2024 at the age of 34 after a malignant stage four tumour was found in his brain. As part of his treatment, brain tissue was removed but only a small amount was kept in a way which could have limited options for further treatment.

His widow, Ellie James, is campaigning for Owain’s Law which would require NHS hospitals freeze suitable brain tumour tissue the right way, and guarantee that the tissue stays the patient’s property under informed consent.

A Change.org petition has been launched, which has already reached over 14,500 signatures.

Mr Evans has also written to the Human Tissue Authority, supported events in Parliament about Owain’s Law, and submitted Written Parliamentary Questions to the UK Government.

Mr. Evans said, “I am looking forward to being able to raise the Owain’s Law campaign in Parliament through a debate in Westminster Hall, where the Government will be able to hear all the points made and provide their response.

“I want to thank the tireless campaigning of Ellie James and everyone else who has contacted me about this important issue.

“I am hopeful that the Government will recognise the powerful points the campaign is making, and commit to changing the law so that people have the widest possible range of options when it comes to cancer treatment.”

The debate will be streamed live on the www.parliamentlive.tv website.

The post Caerphilly MP secures Owain’s Law Debate in Parliament appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP joins Owain’s Law campaign
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, has joined the campaign for Owain’s Law after a meeting with Ellie James, Owain’s widow, in Parliament. Ellie James is campaigning for Owain’s Law which would require NHS hospitals freeze suitable brain tumour tissue the right way, and guarantee that the tissue stays the patient’s property under […]

The post Caerphilly MP joins Owain’s Law campaign appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Evans, the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, has joined the campaign for Owain’s Law after a meeting with Ellie James, Owain’s widow, in Parliament.

Ellie James is campaigning for Owain’s Law which would require NHS hospitals freeze suitable brain tumour tissue the right way, and guarantee that the tissue stays the patient’s property under informed consent.

Owain James, from Caerphilly, died in June 2024 at the age of 34 after a malignant stage four tumour was found in his brain. As part of his treatment, brain tissue was removed but only a small amount was kept in a way which could have limited options for further treatment.

A Change.org petition has been launched by Ellie James, which has already reached over 14,500 signatures.

Mr Evans has vowed to write to the Human Tissue Authority, support events in Parliament supporting Owain’s Law, and submit Written Parliamentary Questions to the UK Government.

Mr. Evans said, “It was a pleasure to meet Ellie in my London office recently to hear about her and Owain’s story, and what I can do to help. Her campaign for Owain’s Law has been tireless and inspiring.

“I think it is right that patients should have some control over how their tissue is kept and what is done with it. In cases of brain tissue, certain kinds of storage can limit options for further treatment which should not be the case.

“I will be raising Owain’s case in Parliament wherever I can, and I will be urging the Government to change the law and give patients more rights over what is done with their tissue.”

The post Caerphilly MP joins Owain’s Law campaign appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Local MP shortlisted for Westminster Book Awards
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
A book authored by the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans MP, has been shortlisted for an award at the tenth Westminster Book Awards. The book, The Football Batallions: The Elite Footballers Who Fought in the Great War, has been shortlisted in the Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian Category. It tells the […]

The post Local MP shortlisted for Westminster Book Awards appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
A book authored by the Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans MP, has been shortlisted for an award at the tenth Westminster Book Awards.

The book, The Football Batallions: The Elite Footballers Who Fought in the Great War, has been shortlisted in the Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian Category.

It tells the extraordinary story of well-known footballers who served in the first world war detailing their individual war stories and, for those who returned, their post-war careers.

The Westminster Book Awards are presented by the Booksellers Association and Publishers Association. They recognise excellent political writing across several categories, including Best Non-Fiction or Fiction by a Parliamentarian, Best Biography by a Parliamentarian, and Best Political Book by a Non-Parliamentarian.

Parliamentarians will now vote to decide the winner in each category, and the results will be announced on 4th February 2026.

Mr. Evans said, “I am incredibly proud to have been shortlisted in the tenth annual Westminster Book Awards.

“It was a great honour to be able to research and tell the stories of these amazing men who served their country with such courage and sacrifice, both in sport and in war.”

“I look forward to the results in February, and I want to wish all the other shortlisted authors the very best of luck.”

The post Local MP shortlisted for Westminster Book Awards appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP welcomes miners pension boost
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has welcomed today’s news in the Autumn Budget that members of the British Coal Staff Superannuation Scheme will receive a pension boost. The Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves MP, announced that the £2.3 billion investment reserve for the BCSSS scheme will be returned to members. Mr. […]

The post Caerphilly MP welcomes miners pension boost appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has welcomed today’s news in the Autumn Budget that members of the British Coal Staff Superannuation Scheme will receive a pension boost.

The Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves MP, announced that the £2.3 billion investment reserve for the BCSSS scheme will be returned to members.

Mr. Evans had previously written to the Chancellor calling for the investment reserve to be returned, after attending a meeting of more than 70 scheme members at the Cascade Community Centre in Penpedairheol in September 2025.

Mr. Evans said, “I am very pleased that the Chancellor has announced the £2.3bn investment reserve will be returned to British Coal Staff Superannuation Scheme members. This is brilliant news.

“Caerphilly has a long and proud mining heritage. Our former miners and coal staff deserve the best possible pension, and this decision by the government should boost what they receive.

“I will continue working with the government on how future surpluses are shared between BCSSS members and the government.”

The post Caerphilly MP welcomes miners pension boost appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Welsh Government urged to adopt Mens Health Strategy by Caerphilly MP
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has urged the Welsh Government to implement a Men’s Health Strategy following the announcement of a UK Government plan focussed on England. Mr. Evans made the comments on 19th November 2025 during a Westminster Hall Debate on the topic of reducing the stigma of suicide. In his […]

The post Welsh Government urged to adopt Mens Health Strategy by Caerphilly MP appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has urged the Welsh Government to implement a Men’s Health Strategy following the announcement of a UK Government plan focussed on England.

Mr. Evans made the comments on 19th November 2025 during a Westminster Hall Debate on the topic of reducing the stigma of suicide. In his contribution, he drew on the recent examples of the late Hefin David MS and boxer Ricky Hatton to call for more action to support men’s mental health.

To coincide with International Men’s Day UK on 19th November, the UK Government announced a 10-year Men’s Health Strategy for England. Mr. Evans has written to Jeremy Miles MS, Cabinet Secretary for Health and Social Care at the Welsh Government, urging a Welsh equivalent of the strategy.

Mr. Evans said: “I am pleased that on International Men’s Day, the UK Government announced a 10-year Men’s Health Strategy for England. I strongly believe that Wales should have an equivalent strategy, and I have written to the Minister urging this.

“Sadly, suicide is the joint-leading cause of death for men in Wales under 50. My constituency has the seventh highest suicide rate in Wales. This is something we do not talk about or get to grips with enough.

“A proper strategy, led by the Welsh Government, NHS Wales, and the many fantastic charities such as Men and Boys, that support men’s mental health, could save lives. We must start by challenging the stigma and stereotypes around men’s mental health, so men who are struggling can get the often life-saving help they need.”

A clip of my contribution in the debate is below.

The post Welsh Government urged to adopt Mens Health Strategy by Caerphilly MP appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP opposes Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has opposed a plan to build a McDonalds Restaurant and Drive-Thru off the A472 in Maesycwmmer. McDonalds ran a pre-application consultation from 5th November – 19th November 2025, which sought feedback from the community on the plans prior to submission to Caerphilly County Borough Council. Mr. Evans […]

The post Caerphilly MP opposes Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has opposed a plan to build a McDonalds Restaurant and Drive-Thru off the A472 in Maesycwmmer.

McDonalds ran a pre-application consultation from 5th November – 19th November 2025, which sought feedback from the community on the plans prior to submission to Caerphilly County Borough Council.

Mr. Evans wrote a letter to the Chief Executive of McDonalds, Lauren Schultz, detailing his opposition on the grounds of traffic congestion on the A472 and A469, potential increases in litter, disruption to local residents, and lack of need.

Mr. Evans said: “Many people have contacted me about McDonalds’ plans to open a new site off the A472 in Maesycwmmer. I think these plans are deeply flawed and I oppose them.

“Anyone who uses that road will know that traffic never flows freely. Adding a McDonalds to this road will only make this problem worse. A McDonalds will also cause disruption to people who have homes very close to the site through litter, light and noise.

“I think McDonalds should reconsider these plans and take the views of my constituents very seriously before taking them any further. Due to the traffic situation, and how close it is to housing, it seems unlikely that this site will ever be suitable for this kind of development.”

The full text of Mr. Evans’ letter is below.


The post Caerphilly MP opposes Maesycwmmer McDonalds Plan appeared first on Chris Evans MP.

item.title: Caerphilly MP recognises ‘light in the darkness’ Ukrainian refugee welcome
item.dc:creator: Office of Chris Evans
item.description:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has today recognised the positive contribution of the Ukrainian refugee community in Caerphilly, and the welcome they have received. He made his remarks at the closing ceremony of a solo exhibition by Ukrainian artist and poet Olesia Miftahova at Ty Penallta, Caerphilly County Borough Council’s headquarters. The […]

The post Caerphilly MP recognises ‘light in the darkness’ Ukrainian refugee welcome appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
item.content:encoded:
The Member of Parliament for Caerphilly, Chris Evans, has today recognised the positive contribution of the Ukrainian refugee community in Caerphilly, and the welcome they have received.

He made his remarks at the closing ceremony of a solo exhibition by Ukrainian artist and poet Olesia Miftahova at Ty Penallta, Caerphilly County Borough Council’s headquarters.

The exhibition, titled A Welsh Afternoon, concluded with a tour of the exhibits, musical performances by members of Gwent Baroque, and a reading of Welsh poetry.

Mr Evans had previously written to Caerphilly County Borough Council asking for the Council to host the exhibition at Ty Penallta.

Mr. Evans said: “The warm welcome the people of Caerphilly have given to the Ukrainian refugees in our communities has been a light in the darkness, in what has otherwise been an horrific situation in Europe.

“The message our communities have given is simple. We stand with the people of Ukraine against aggression, and we are honoured to have been able to provide them a home when they needed it most.

“I want to pay tribute to the work of Olesia Miftahova and so many others in Caerphilly’s Ukrainian community for embracing our culture and sharing theirs with us. I am delighted to have two of her artworks for my London office.”

The post Caerphilly MP recognises ‘light in the darkness’ Ukrainian refugee welcome appeared first on Chris Evans MP.
